summary: Weapon SFX, title/gameplay music, Glyph voice, and Commander Kade intro VO are committed as generated MP3 assets and routed through the shared gesture-unlocked Web Audio manager.
observations: Previous gun sounds were procedural Web Audio and not ElevenLabs assets.
Generated three weapon SFX assets with the ElevenLabs text-to-sound-effects endpoint.
Generated one 24 second instrumental foundation loop with the ElevenLabs music stream endpoint.
Runtime browser code loads committed public MP3 assets through publicAssetUrl and never receives an ElevenLabs API key.
Audio asset prompts, endpoints, models, settings, bytes, hashes, LUFS targets, and intended use are recorded in docs/assets/source-ledger.json and source-metadata.json.
VAULT keeps the same generated MP3 file, but runtime playback gain and source metadata volume were raised to 1.8 because the heavy pulse sounded too quiet.
Generated RIFT precision and TORCH burst SFX through the local secret broker.
Replaced the old 24 second music loop with foundation-combat-loop-long.mp3 at 48 seconds.
Runtime audio now preloads six committed MP3 assets.
Runtime weapon SFX pools are now created immediately from the manifest, before async byte verification completes, so an early first shot has a playable SFX element available.
Audio debug snapshots now report sfxPoolProfiles, playRequests, and missedPlayRequests so smoke QA can catch a shot that had no SFX pool candidate.
Weapon SFX now decode into AudioBuffers after the first user gesture and use Web Audio one-shot sources for held-fire playback.
The HTML audio pool remains as a pre-decode fallback and reports htmlFallbackPlayRequests.
Enemy projectile SFX currently reuses the committed ElevenLabs RIFT precision MP3 at lower gain and playback rate for an MVP projectile cue.
Latest npm run validate:browser passed all five landscape viewport projects with decoded profiles, Web Audio shot playback, zero SFX play failures, and projectile sound request coverage.
Foundation music now uses the Web Audio graph instead of HTMLAudioElement playback: the MP3 is fetched, byte-verified, decoded after unlock, and looped with a dedicated gain node.
Audio load verification runs weapon SFX and music checks through Promise.all; actual overlap comes from independent Web Audio source nodes mixed by one AudioContext.
Browser smoke now checks musicDecoded and musicPlaying so losing the music loop is caught.
Title music, gameplay music, weapon SFX, enemy projectile SFX, and Glyph voice lines now share one gesture-unlocked runtime audio manager.
The previous gameplay death voice path used a separate HTMLAudioElement pool, so death barks could work from the debug button while failing during real gameplay timing.
Character Voice Lab playback now routes through the same runtime audio manager as gameplay voices and reports active voice state through the debug snapshot.
Runtime audio snapshots now report active voice asset ID, decoded voice asset IDs, and voice play request count so smoke QA can catch missing voice playback.
Cloudflare builds now run the same dist preparation step used for Pages, which prunes source-only character GLBs before publishing.
Generated a new 90.04 second ElevenLabs music_v1 title loop at title-industrial-guitar-loop.mp3 with original industrial guitar arcade energy.
The previous title loop was removed from public runtime assets so Cloudflare does not ship unused title music.
Title music runtime gain was raised from 0.3 to 0.46 because the prior intro mix felt too soft.
The title hero now uses the regular idle animation clip instead of idle-alt; idle-alt remains available in character debug.
Generated five Commander Kade intro VO MP3s with ElevenLabs eleven_v3 and voice id gVh6lddROTbOaOz9AAnY.
Commander intro VO is preloaded with GAME_VOICE_LINES and decoded through the shared voice path.
Voice Lab now previews both Glyph and Commander Kade lines.
Voice Lab smoke uses a longer Commander Kade HOSTILES line for activeVoiceAssetId checks so the assertion does not race short Glyph clip completion.
decisions: Do not expose ElevenLabs API keys in browser code.
Generate server-side or build-time and cache audio as assets.
Use committed MP3 assets for the MVP foundation audio payload.
Unlock music and SFX from a user gesture, then let the player mute/unmute music from the HUD.
Treat generated ElevenLabs audio as provider-generated output with metadata validation, not as CC0 external art.
Use runtime gain tweaks for small mix fixes when the source file, hash, and bytes do not change.
Use the 48 second music loop as the default foundation music asset.
Keep all ElevenLabs API use build-time/local only; browser runtime loads committed cache-busted MP3s.
Keep audio byte verification as the provenance/load gate, but do not block creation of local SFX playback pools on that async check.
Prefer decoded Web Audio buffer playback for repeated combat SFX; keep HTMLAudioElement pools only as fallback.
Generate a dedicated ElevenLabs enemy projectile/impact SFX set later instead of treating the reused RIFT cue as final.
Use Web Audio for both looping music and one-shot SFX so all combat audio shares one unlock and mix path.
Keep all runtime music, SFX, and voice playback on one AudioContext unlock path after user interaction.
Decode voice MP3s into Web Audio buffers and stop the separate death-voice HTML media pool.
Keep the Voice Lab as the player-facing audition page, but make it exercise the same manager APIs used by gameplay.
Only music decode completion should attempt to start a loop; SFX and voice decode should not re-enter the music startup path.
Disconnect one-shot Web Audio SFX nodes on ended so repeated held-fire playback does not retain finished nodes.
Use an original heavy industrial guitar prompt for the new title loop and do not request imitation of a specific commercial soundtrack.
Keep title music at MP3 44.1 kHz 128 kbps so the replacement stays within the existing title audio size class.
Commander cinematic VO must remain committed public MP3 assets generated through the local secret broker.
Voice Lab should exercise gameplay/cinematic voice playback paths instead of page-local audio elements.
caughtIssues: The current browser autoplay model means music must not be expected to play before a user gesture.
The asset validator needed a generated-audio source path instead of a CC0-only source gate.
The earlier SFX preload path created weapon audio pools only after async fetch verification, so very early shots could miss sound until a later cadence shot.
Small HTML media pools can cut off or delay rapid overlapping shots on mobile when held-fire cadence overlaps.
HTMLAudioElement.play can fail or remain silent under gesture/autoplay edge cases, so music should stay on the same Web Audio unlock path as SFX.
A debug-only death trigger was not enough coverage because it did not reproduce the same timing as projectile-driven player death.
Voice playback had no debug snapshot counters before this slice, so a missing death bark was hard to distinguish from a muted device or browser autoplay block.
Publishing all public source assets to Cloudflare would ship roughly 136 MB; prepared dist is roughly 16 MB after pruning source-only character assets.
Shipping both old and new title loops would add about 1.44 MB of unused deploy payload, so the old title MP3 was removed.
risks: The 24 second music loop may become repetitive during longer playthroughs.
Generated output usage must continue to follow the active ElevenLabs account and terms.
SFX loudness is metadata-targeted but not yet measured by an automated LUFS tool.
Enemy projectile sound identity is temporary because it reuses an existing ElevenLabs weapon cue.
The centralized audio manager still lives behind the weapon system facade for this MVP; a later architecture pass may move it to a top-level game service.
